front: What is the copper-catalyzed azide-alkyne cycloaddition (CuAAC) click reaction?
back: CuAAC forms 1,4-disubstituted 1,2,3-triazole from azide and terminal alkyne using Cu(I) catalyst. Reaction is regiospecific, high-yielding, and tolerates many functional groups. Copper ligands (TBTA) prevent Cu aggregation. Used extensively in bioconjugation.
